_license: Original content for Open Dungeon Master. The pick-lists 5e hands out alongside class features: invocations, maneuvers, metamagic, pact boons, infusions, runes and elemental disciplines. Each line states the option's mechanics in our own wording; no publisher's descriptive text is reproduced. See docs/LICENSES.md.
_note: Before this file these were not choices at all. A warlock's sheet said 'Eldritch Invocations' and stopped, so the player never picked any and never got the abilities. A pick is stored on the sheet as a choice-sourced feature named with the kind's prefix ('Invocation: Agonizing Blast'), which is exactly how Fighting Style already worked, so populateFeatures preserves it across level-ups with no schema change.
_shape: kinds[kind] = { label, prefix, grantedBy: the feature name that unlocks the picks }. options[] = { k: kind, n: name, d: rules text, req?: prerequisite note }. Counts live in src/lib/srd/options.ts.

options.d: Add your Charisma modifier to the damage of each Eldritch Blast beam. Usually the first invocation a warlock takes.
Cast mage armor on yourself at will, without a spell slot.
Cast levitate on yourself at will, without a spell slot.
You no longer need to sleep and cannot be forced to. You keep watch through the whole night.
Cast speak with animals at will, without a spell slot.
You gain proficiency in Deception and Persuasion.
Cast compulsion once per long rest using a warlock spell slot.
Inscribe two 1st-level rituals in your Book of Shadows and cast them as rituals. You can add more rituals you find.
Cast hold monster at will on a celestial, fiend or elemental, without a slot, once per creature per long rest.
A bonus action wraps you in buzzing flies for a minute: advantage on Intimidation, disadvantage on other Charisma checks, and poison damage to creatures that end their turn within 5 feet.
You see normally in darkness, magical and nonmagical, out to 120 feet. Pairs famously with casting darkness on yourself.
Cast confusion once per long rest using a warlock spell slot.
You have advantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration on a spell.
Cast detect magic at will, without a spell slot.
Your Eldritch Blast reaches 300 feet instead of 120.
You can read all writing.
Cast false life on yourself at will as a 1st-level spell, without a slot.
Touch a willing humanoid and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn, extendable each turn.
As an action, see through solid objects out to 30 feet for a minute, once per short or long rest.
You can breathe underwater, gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed, and cast water breathing once per long rest without a slot.
Whenever you regain hit points while your familiar is within 100 feet, you regain the maximum possible.
Once per turn when Eldritch Blast hits, you can pull the target 10 feet toward you.
Your pact weapon counts as magical, gives +1 to attack and damage, and can serve as your spellcasting focus. You can also make a bow or crossbow your pact weapon.
Your familiar gains a flying or swimming speed, its attacks count as magical, it can be commanded as a bonus action, and it can impose your spell save DC on its effects.
Your pact weapon deals extra necrotic damage equal to your Charisma modifier on a hit.
A bonus action deals psychic damage equal to your Charisma modifier to your hex target and every creature within 5 feet of it.
options.req: level 5, hex or a curse feature

options.d: Cast disguise self at will, without a spell slot.
Cast alter self at will, without a spell slot.
Cast conjure elemental once per long rest using a warlock spell slot.
Cast slow once per long rest using a warlock spell slot.
Cast silent image at will, without a spell slot.
In dim light or darkness, become invisible as an action until you move or act.
Cast jump on yourself at will, without a spell slot.
The wearer of your talisman can add a d4 to a failed saving throw, a proficiency-bonus number of times per long rest.
When the wearer of your talisman is hit, you can use a reaction to deal psychic damage equal to your proficiency bonus and push the attacker 10 feet.
A bonus action teleports you up to 30 feet to a space near your cursed target.
options.req: level 7, hex or a curse feature

options.d: Once per turn when Eldritch Blast hits, you can push the target 10 feet away. Excellent battlefield control.
Cast polymorph once per long rest using a warlock spell slot.
Cast invisibility at will, without a spell slot.
Cast bestow curse once per long rest using a warlock spell slot.
Cast bane once per long rest using a warlock spell slot.
You attack twice with your pact weapon whenever you take the Attack action.
As a reaction on taking damage, encase yourself in ice for temporary hit points, at the cost of being restrained and taking cold damage; once per short or long rest.
Cast freedom of movement on yourself once per long rest, without a slot.
Cast arcane eye at will, without a spell slot.
You can speak through your familiar, perceive through its senses, and communicate with it at any distance on the same plane.
Cast speak with dead at will, without a spell slot.
You see the true form of any shapechanger or creature concealed by illusion within 30 feet.
You can conjure a magical weapon of any kind as an action, and it counts as magical. This is the melee warlock's pact.
You learn find familiar and can take special forms such as an imp, quasit, pseudodragon or sprite, and your familiar can attack with its own reaction.
You gain a Book of Shadows holding three cantrips from any class's list, which you can cast at will.
You gain an amulet that lets its wearer add a d4 to a failed ability check, a proficiency-bonus number of times per long rest.
Spend a die to add it to a Stealth check or an initiative roll.
Spend a die to swap places with a willing ally within 5 feet, and one of you adds the die to your AC until your next turn.
As a reaction when a creature moves into your reach, spend a die to attack it and add the die to the damage.
Give up one of your attacks and spend a die to let an ally use their reaction to attack, adding the die to their damage.
Spend a die to add it to an Intimidation, Performance or Persuasion check.
Spend a die to add it to the damage and force a Strength save, dropping one item the target is holding.
Spend a die to add it to the damage and give the next attacker advantage against the target.
Spend a die and add it to your AC while you move.
A bonus action feints at a creature within 5 feet, giving you advantage on your next attack against it and adding the die to the damage.
Spend a die to add it to the damage and force a Wisdom save, giving the target disadvantage on attacks against anyone but you.
After hitting, spend a die as a bonus action to attempt a grapple, adding the die to your check.
Spend a die to reach 5 feet further with a melee attack and add the die to the damage.
Spend a die to add it to the damage and let an ally move half their speed without provoking from the target.
Spend a die to add it to the damage and frighten the target on a failed Wisdom save.
As a reaction, spend a die to reduce melee damage to you by the die plus your Dexterity modifier.
Spend a die to add it to an attack roll, before or after seeing whether it hits.
Spend a die to add it to the damage and push a Large or smaller target 15 feet on a failed Strength save.
A bonus action draws and throws a weapon, spending a die to add it to the damage.
A bonus action grants an ally temporary hit points equal to the die plus your Charisma modifier.
As a reaction when a creature misses you in melee, spend a die to attack it and add the die to the damage.
After hitting, spend a die to deal that much damage to a second creature within 5 feet of the first.
Spend a die to add it to a History, Insight or Investigation check.
Spend a die to add it to the damage and knock a Large or smaller target prone on a failed Strength save.
Spend 1 sorcery point to let a number of creatures equal to your Charisma modifier automatically succeed on the spell's saving throw.
Spend 1 sorcery point to double a spell's range, or give a touch spell 30 feet of reach.
Spend 1 sorcery point to reroll damage dice equal to your Charisma modifier.
Spend 1 sorcery point to double a spell's duration, up to 24 hours.
Spend 3 sorcery points to give one target disadvantage on its first saving throw against the spell.
Spend 2 sorcery points to cast a one-action spell as a bonus action instead.
Spend 2 sorcery points to reroll a missed attack roll for a spell.
Spend 1 sorcery point to cast without verbal or somatic components, which no one can see or hear you do.
Spend 1 sorcery point to change a spell's damage type among acid, cold, fire, lightning, poison and thunder.
Spend sorcery points equal to the spell's level to have a single-target spell hit a second creature.
Armor that raises the wearer's speed by 5 feet, grants a returning gauntlet attack, and replaces missing limbs.
Armor that lets the wearer use Intelligence for Strength checks and saves, and spend charges to add to a save or avoid being knocked prone.
Boots that let the wearer teleport back to a space they occupied earlier in the turn.
A focus granting +1 to spell attack rolls and letting spells ignore half cover.
Armor or a shield with a +1 bonus to AC, rising to +2 at higher levels.
A weapon with a +1 bonus to attack and damage, rising to +2 at higher levels.
A helm granting advantage on initiative rolls.
A tiny construct companion that acts on your turn, scouts, and channels your spells.
A garment with charges that turn a failed concentration save into a success.
A weapon that sheds light, grants +1 to attack and damage, and can blind an attacker as a reaction.
A ranged weapon with +1 to attack and damage that produces its own ammunition.
Reproduce a common magic item from the artificer's replicable list; the list grows with your level.
A shield with +1 AC and charges that push an attacker 15 feet away.
Armor granting resistance to one damage type of your choice.
A thrown weapon with +1 to attack and damage that flies back to the wielder's hand.
A ring that restores one expended spell slot of 3rd level or lower once per day.
Advantage on Sleight of Hand and Deception. Invoke it as a reaction to redirect an attack from one creature to another within 30 feet.
Doubled proficiency on tool checks. Invoke it on a hit for extra fire damage and to bind the target in fiery chains, restraining it.
Advantage on Animal Handling and Intimidation. Invoke it for a bonus to Strength and Constitution checks and saves for 10 minutes.
Advantage on saving throws against poison and resistance to poison damage. Invoke it to gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing and slashing damage for a minute.
Advantage on Insight and darkvision to 120 feet. Invoke it as a reaction to charm a creature within 30 feet that fails a Wisdom save.
Advantage on Arcana and you cannot be surprised while conscious. Invoke it to see glimpses of the future, granting advantage or imposing disadvantage on rolls near you.
Spend 6 ki to cast cone of cold.
Spend 3 ki to cast hold person.
A minor elemental trick: snuff or light a flame, chill or warm an object, or shape a Medium mass of earth, fire, water or mist for a moment.
Spend 5 ki to cast stoneskin on yourself.
Spend 1 ki when you take the Attack action to add 10 feet of reach and fire damage to your unarmed strikes.
Spend 2 ki to cast thunderwave.
Spend 2 ki to strike a creature within 30 feet for 3d10 bludgeoning damage, pushing and knocking it prone on a failed Strength save.
Spend 4 ki to cast fireball.
Spend 3 ki to cast shatter.
Spend 4 ki to cast gaseous form on yourself.
Spend 4 ki to cast fly on yourself.
Spend 5 ki to cast wall of fire.
Spend 2 ki to cast gust of wind.
Spend 1 ki to freeze, thaw or reshape a 30-foot area of water or ice.
Spend 2 ki to cast burning hands.
Spend 2 ki to lash a creature within 30 feet for 3d10 bludgeoning damage, then pull it 25 feet or knock it prone on a failed Dexterity save.
Spend 6 ki to cast wall of stone.
